description: Create a lightweight Clash Verge rules sync solution with a personal GitHub repository, GitHub Actions auto-sync workflow, and a Mihomo-compatible configuration example that stays updated from upstream sources.

userStories.title: 创建最小规则聚合仓库模板
userStories.description: As a Clash Verge user, I want a minimal GitHub repository template so that I can quickly create my own rules sync repository without designing the structure from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide a clear repository structure description
Repository includes README.md, .github/workflows/update-rules.yml, config/clash-verge-rules.example.yaml, and a rules/ placeholder directory
README explains sync sources, setup steps, and maintenance approach
File paths and naming stay consistent across the document
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 自动同步上游基础规则
userStories.description: As a Clash Verge user, I want the repository to automatically sync core base rules so that I do not have to manually download and update common direct, proxy, and blocking rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow syncs private, reject, direct, proxy, lancidr, and cncidr
Sync source is Loyalsoldier/clash-rules release branch
Each file is downloaded and saved unchanged into the rules/ directory
GitHub Actions job fails when any download fails
No secondary processing is applied to these base rules
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 自动同步专项服务规则
userStories.description: As a Clash Verge user who needs fine-grained routing, I want the repository to sync a small set of service-specific rules so that I can control key services while keeping the setup lightweight.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow syncs OpenAI, Anthropic, Claude, Copilot, Telegram, Netflix, Disney, and Advertising
Sync source is blackmatrix7/ios_rule_script release branch under rule/Clash
Rules are stored as original YAML files in the rules/ directory
All service rule URLs are documented or directly traceable from the workflow
No YAML parsing, rewriting, merging, or filtering is applied
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 定时同步并自动提交变更
userStories.description: As a repository maintainer, I want GitHub Actions to sync and commit rule updates on a schedule so that the repository stays current without manual intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow supports both manual trigger and scheduled trigger
Workflow runs at least once per day by default
When files in rules/ change, the workflow commits and pushes updates automatically
When nothing changes, the workflow exits without creating an empty commit
Workflow only requires contents: write permission
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 提供 Clash Verge 可直接接入的配置示例
userStories.description: As a Clash Verge user, I want a configuration example that points to my own repository so that I can quickly consume the synced rule files.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide a rule-providers configuration example
Provide a matching rules ordering example
All URLs use placeholders for the user's own GitHub repository
Behavior types are explicitly set and match the file formats
Configuration clearly assumes an existing PROXY group
Configuration is documented as Clash Verge / Mihomo only
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 支持多设备统一使用同一规则源
userStories.description: As a user with multiple devices, I want every device to point to the same repository URLs so that I do not have to maintain rule sources separately per device.
acceptanceCriteria: PRD requires all devices to use the same set of URLs from the user's repository
Documentation explains the update chain as upstream repositories to personal repository to Clash Verge clients
The solution does not require storing upstream raw files separately on each client
The solution does not require running sync scripts on each device
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 降低后续维护成本
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want adding, removing, or switching rule sources to require minimal edits so that the solution stays easy to maintain over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding a rule only requires editing the workflow and relevant documentation
Removing a rule does not require changing any complex generation logic
Switching an upstream URL does not require restructuring the repository
The PRD explicitly forbids expanding this solution into a complex rule generation system
Typecheck passes
